evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` combines client access, singleton lifecycle, instrumentation install, and runtime initialization in one canonical entrypoint (`getSupabaseClient` -> `buildSupabaseClient`), making side effects implicit at access time.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` is a shared error helper that also triggers user-facing notification side effects (`notifyError`) rather than staying purely normalization/logging-focused.

Modal/dialog ownership is split across multiple ad-hoc component implementations with repeated control contracts (`open`, `onOpenChange`) in `src/shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx`, `src/pages/Home/components/InstallInstructionsModal.tsx`, and `src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/components/DescriptionModal.tsx`.

Type ownership for generation entities is blurred by re-export indirection (`src/types/generationRow.ts`) while domain types are also defined under `src/domains/generation/types` and consumed from there (`src/domains/generation/types/shot.ts`).

Lightbox flow is split across `ImageLightbox.tsx`, `VideoLightbox.tsx`, and `useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` but still passes very large grouped prop surfaces (`navigation`, `shotWorkflow`, `features`, `actions`, plus nested callback bags), so wrappers add indirection without reducing interface size.

`taskParamsUtils.ts` mixes multiple abstraction layers: low-level typed coercion helpers, scope resolution (`createTaskParamScopes`), and high-level view models (`buildTaskParamsViewModel`, `buildVideoTravelTaskParamsViewModel`, `buildImageTaskParamsViewModel`) with many fallback chains across the same keys.

Task creation modules (`joinClips.ts`, `individualTravelSegment.ts`, `travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`) each re-encode large optional param bags and partial inheritance logic rather than sharing a smaller boundary contract.

`src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts` exposes `OperationResult`-based APIs, while `src/shared/lib/generationTaskQueries.ts` immediately converts the same calls into throwing APIs, creating two incompatible error handoff styles for one boundary.

`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeOperation.ts` defines failure shape with `errorCode`, but `supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts` serializes `result.code`, so shared edge error contracts are not consistently translated at the HTTP boundary.

`src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts` includes a `selectShotForAddition` event payload `{ shotId, shotName }`, while `src/shared/contexts/ShotAdditionSelectionContext.tsx` state/action API only tracks `shotId`, so sibling integration seams expose different selection contracts.

`src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipManager/metadata.ts` repeats the same dual-surface pattern (`loadClipDurationResult` + throwing `loadClipDuration`) seen in generation task mapping APIs, increasing adapter glue at call sites instead of enforcing one boundary style.

`individualTravelSegment.ts` has very long local control flow with many helper branches (`buildSegmentState`, `buildOrchestratorDetails`, `buildTaskParamsPayload`) and repeated conditional field assembly, increasing local reasoning cost.
`joinClips.ts` builds `orchestratorDetails` through a large mutable object plus many `setPayloadFieldIf*` calls and per-join override branching, making behavior hard to audit.
`useSegmentSlotMode.ts` contains many responsibilities (deep-link parsing, structure-video overlap resolution, mode data synthesis, frame update routing) in one hook file, reducing internal clarity.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelinePositions.ts contains heavy banner/comment scaffolding (multi-section separators and long narrative blocks) that restates implementation details rather than compactly documenting intent.
src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/useClickOutside.ts embeds tutorial-style usage examples and verbose inline docs for a small hook, adding maintenance noise in production code.
supabase/functions/ai-prompt/index.ts parses `const _temperature = Number(body.temperature ?? 0.7);` but never uses it, indicating migration residue/placeholder logic.
`checkOrchestratorCompletion` declares `taskIdString` but never reads it in the function body (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:72-95`).
Caller still threads `taskIdString` into the unused parameter (`supabase/functions/complete_task/index.ts:354-361`), which adds dead control/data flow to a critical completion path.
logic_clarity.unreported_risk: The reviewed cross-cutting files are broad; additional subtle clarity issues may exist in unflagged branches that are behaviorally correct but harder to simplify without deeper runtime-path analysis.

findings.summary: High-traffic shared entrypoints mix access APIs with runtime side effects, obscuring subsystem ownership and startup behavior.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` installs instrumentation and initializes runtime inside `buildSupabaseClient()` (lines 12-20) but exposes it as a plain accessor (`getSupabaseClient`, lines 36-37).
`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` performs normalization/logging and also conditionally triggers toast UI (`notifyError`) in the same helper (lines 19-27).
Both files are listed in the blind packet as heavily imported hubs, so hidden side effects here propagate architecture-wide.
findings.suggestion: Split these into explicit bootstrap APIs and pure access/util APIs: e.g., a one-time app initialization module for Supabase instrumentation/runtime and an error pipeline where UI notification is a separate opt-in adapter from normalization/logging.

findings.summary: Generation row types have two apparent ownership locations, weakening domain boundary clarity.
evidence: `src/types/generationRow.ts` is only a re-export shim to `@/domains/generation/types/generationRow` (line 1), creating an additional top-level entrypoint for the same domain type.
`src/domains/generation/types/shot.ts` imports `GenerationRow` directly from the domain-local path (`./generationRow`, line 1), so both global and domain-local import styles are valid in-repo.
findings.suggestion: Choose one canonical import boundary for generation types (prefer domain-local public index) and deprecate/remove the top-level shim to prevent ongoing drift in import conventions.

findings.summary: Dialog behavior is implemented via repeated local patterns instead of a coherent modal architecture boundary.
evidence: All three components expose and wire their own `open`/`onOpenChange` contracts (`ConfirmDialog` lines 35-40 and 100; `InstallInstructionsModal` lines 7-10 and 41; `DescriptionModal` lines 6-8 and 13).
`ConfirmDialog` includes bespoke close suppression state (`suppressNextCloseRef`, lines 78-84), indicating local lifecycle behavior that is not standardized across other modals.
findings.suggestion: Define a shared modal interaction contract (state transitions, confirm/cancel semantics, close suppression rules) and implement feature modals via that shared boundary to reduce ownership fragmentation.

findings.summary: Lightbox abstractions add wrapper layers but keep a very large, duplicated prop/callback surface.
evidence: `ImageLightboxProps` and `VideoLightboxProps` both carry large grouped bags (`navigation`, `shotWorkflow`, `features`, `actions`) and then rehydrate them into additional layout/workflow props.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` reconstructs another aggregated state object (`lightboxStateValue`, `layoutProps`, `workflow*Props`) rather than reducing interface breadth.
`LightboxLayout` consumes many nested props and context values simultaneously, showing interface size remains high despite added intermediates.
findings.suggestion: Define a single typed lightbox controller contract (core state + command methods) consumed by layouts/components, and move media-specific differences behind a small strategy object to remove repeated prop-bag mapping.

findings.summary: Task param extraction abstraction is overloaded with multiple layers and repeated fallback semantics.
evidence: `taskParamsUtils.ts` keeps both direct derive functions (`derivePrompt`, `deriveNegativePrompt`) and scope-based resolvers (`pickScoped*`) plus multiple view model builders in one module.
Many fields are resolved through long fallback chains across top-level/orchestrator/segment locations, indicating schema ambiguity is absorbed repeatedly in code.
UI detail components rely on these broad derived models, so ambiguity at extraction layer propagates into rendering contracts.
findings.suggestion: Split into explicit schema adapters per task family (image, travel, join) that normalize once into a canonical DTO, then keep UI view models as thin presentational transforms.

findings.summary: Generation completion routing depends on implicit payload conventions shared across distant modules.
evidence: `createGenerationFromTask` branches on flags like `skip_generation`, `based_on`, `parent_generation_id`, `child_generation_id` that are produced by task creators as plain params.

Task creators write overlapping lineage/orchestration data to multiple payload locations (`orchestration_contract`, `orchestrator_details`, top-level fields), requiring consumer-side knowledge of producer behavior.
`checkOrchestratorCompletion` and generation routing each interpret orchestration context differently, increasing seam fragility when payload shapes evolve.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a versioned shared task-lineage contract module (single canonical location for routing fields) used by both task creation and complete_task consumers, with parser/validator guards at boundaries.

findings.summary: Shot segment callbacks mix domain mutations with broad cache choreography, creating brittle handoff seams.
evidence: `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` performs DB reads, sibling discovery, deletion, direct query data rewrites, and multiple invalidations in one callback path.
`useTasks` similarly mixes query execution with freshness side effects (`onFetchFailure/onFetchSuccess`) and pagination/sorting behavior.
Freshness and cache concerns are spread across hooks rather than isolated behind a dedicated data-access seam.
findings.suggestion: Move mutation + cache/freshness orchestration into dedicated repository/service hooks (single command per domain action) so UI callbacks call one boundary operation instead of coordinating multiple query systems.

findings.summary: Task payload builders have high branch density and mutable assembly patterns that reduce local readability.
evidence: `joinClips.ts` uses a large mutable `orchestratorDetails` object with many conditional field setters and per-join override mutation paths.
`individualTravelSegment.ts` combines state derivation, inheritance stripping, mode normalization, and payload assembly with many intertwined helper branches.
`payloadBuilder.ts` and `imageGeneration.ts` repeat similar optional-field expansion and mode-dependent payload composition patterns.
findings.suggestion: Refactor payload creation into small pure sections (required core, mode-specific, optional overlays) returning immutable fragments, then compose with explicit precedence order and per-section tests.

findings.summary: Generation-task lookup seam exposes both Result-based and throw-based APIs for the same operation, forcing inconsistent caller integration paths.
evidence: `getPrimaryTaskIdForGenerationResult` and `getPrimaryTaskIdsForGenerationsResult` return `OperationResult` in repository layer.
`generationTaskQueries.ts` wraps those functions and throws on `!result.ok`, changing contract shape at the next layer.
`generationTaskCache.ts` then must use try/catch around `getPrimaryTaskIdsForGenerations`, even though lower layer already had typed failure signaling.
findings.suggestion: Standardize this boundary on one contract style (prefer `OperationResult` end-to-end in shared libs, with throw conversion only at UI entry points), then update task cache callers to consume that single shape directly.

findings.summary: Shared edge failure objects and HTTP error serialization disagree on field names, causing unstable API error payloads.
evidence: `EdgeFailureResult` defines `errorCode` in `edgeOperation.ts`.
`toErrorResponse` in `edgeRequest.ts` serializes `error: result.code` instead of `result.errorCode`.
Current tests validate status behavior but do not assert serialized error body key/value, so this contract mismatch can pass unnoticed.
findings.suggestion: Unify on `errorCode` across all edge helpers and responses; patch serializer to use `result.errorCode` and add explicit test assertions for response JSON error code fields.

findings.summary: Shot-selection handoff uses inconsistent payload shape between event bus and context API.
evidence: `typedEvents.ts` event `selectShotForAddition` requires `{ shotId: string; shotName: string }`.
`ShotAdditionSelectionContext` only supports `selectShotForAddition(shotId: string)` and stores only `selectedShotId`.
These parallel seams encode different minimum data requirements for the same action, increasing mapper/adapter burden and drift risk.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical shot-selection contract (either ID-only or ID+name) and align both event and context APIs to that shared type.

findings.summary: Duplicate Result-plus-throw wrapper patterns across modules create avoidable boundary indirection and inconsistent orchestration style.
evidence: `clipManager/metadata.ts` exposes `loadClipDurationResult` and then `loadClipDuration` that throws on failure.
`generationTaskQueries.ts` similarly wraps repository Result APIs and throws.
The same seam pattern appears in separate subsystems without a shared rule for when Result is preserved vs converted, which makes integration behavior less predictable.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a repository/service boundary rule: keep `OperationResult` in shared service layers and perform throw conversion only in feature-specific UI hooks/components.

findings.summary: Excessive explanatory scaffolding adds AI-style noise and obscures the executable logic.
evidence: useTimelinePositions.ts includes repeated section banners and long explanatory comments around straightforward guard clauses and callback wiring.
useClickOutside.ts includes multiple usage examples and long JSDoc blocks for a short hook whose behavior is already obvious from signature and implementation.
supabaseSession.ts includes extensive operational narrative in module header and function docs, with several comments restating what 1-2 lines of code already show.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to intent/rationale only: keep one short module note per file, remove decorative separators, and move usage examples to docs/tests instead of runtime source.

findings.summary: Unused request parameter indicates incomplete provider migration path.
evidence: ai-prompt/index.ts computes `_temperature` in `enhance_segment_prompt` but never uses it in the OpenAI request payload.
The same handler mixes two provider styles (Groq SDK branches and raw OpenAI HTTP branch), suggesting transitional code where controls are inconsistently applied.
findings.suggestion: Either wire `temperature` into the OpenAI request (if supported/desired) or remove the field and request parsing entirely; also normalize provider-call option handling across branches to eliminate dead migration residue.

findings.summary: One hook owns too many concerns and uses repeated linear lookups in a persistence hot path.
evidence: useTimelinePositions.ts combines DB sync policy, optimistic updates, rollback, lock state, status state machine, endpoint persistence, and UI notification in one ~700-line unit.
Inside `updatePositions`, each change calls `shotGenerations.find(...)`, causing repeated scans before building DB updates.
Neighboring timeline hooks/constants are small and focused, highlighting the contrast and concentration of mutable behavior in this single file.
findings.suggestion: Split `useTimelinePositions` into focused helpers (sync guard, optimistic state manager, persistence adapter), and prebuild a `Map<string, GenerationRow>` for O(1) lookup before iterating changes.

findings.summary: Large switch branch duplication makes request handling harder to evolve safely.
evidence: ai-prompt/index.ts repeats parse/validate/call/response patterns across `generate_prompts`, `edit_prompt`, `generate_summary`, and `enhance_segment_prompt` with near-identical error handling structure.
The branch body interleaves transport concerns, prompt construction, and response shaping, increasing local complexity per case.
findings.suggestion: Extract per-task handlers into a task map with shared request parsing/error wrapper; keep provider-specific call builders isolated so each branch only defines task-specific inputs/outputs.

findings.summary: A shared hook entrypoint is owned by a feature module, reversing expected layer direction.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useCredits.ts` exports directly from `@/features/billing/hooks/useCredits` instead of owning shared behavior.
The colocated shared test imports `../useCredits` and exercises billing-specific behavior, so package placement implies shared ownership for feature-specific logic.
findings.suggestion: Choose one owner and align paths: either move the hook/tests into `features/billing` and remove the shared alias, or create a true shared contract with feature-specific adapters outside `shared/hooks`.

findings.summary: The `shared/lib` directory mixes unrelated concerns (domain constants, UI sizing helpers, and dev-only browser monitoring).
evidence: `aspectRatios.ts` combines core ratio-to-resolution mapping with UI preview dimension helpers in one utility file.
`autoplayMonitor.ts` is browser-only and gated by `import.meta.env.DEV`, but it is placed beside general-purpose shared utilities.
`ProjectScopedCache.ts` is generic infrastructure utility, further indicating `shared/lib` is a catch-all instead of domain-oriented packages.
findings.suggestion: Split `shared/lib` into intention-revealing subpackages (for example `shared/domain/media`, `shared/runtime/devtools`, `shared/infrastructure/cache`) and move these files to match ownership.

findings.summary: Error-handling runtime contract is documented in README but not co-located with the canonical error API module.
evidence: README Runtime Contracts specifies notifier readiness semantics and bootstrap registration behavior.
`handleError.ts` exports only `handleError` and option typing; the documented contract elements are not visible from this ownership entry point.
findings.suggestion: Move authoritative contract details into module-level docs/types near `handleError` (or export a small contract surface from the same boundary), and keep README as a high-level pointer instead of the primary contract source.

findings.summary: The GenerationRow compatibility bridge is governed by README policy only, without in-code migration pressure.
evidence: README states `src/types/generationRow.ts` is temporary and domain modules should import from domain types.
`src/types/generationRow.ts` is a plain re-export and contains no deprecation tag, lint boundary hint, or ownership warning.
findings.suggestion: Add explicit deprecation metadata/comments and enforce import boundaries (lint rule or codemod policy) so the compatibility layer has an executable migration contract.

findings.summary: Supabase client governance is primarily README-driven rather than encoded as an explicit contract at the integration boundary.
evidence: README defines canonical accessor usage, legacy proxy isolation, and debug-global behavior as architecture policy.
`client.ts` implements lazy singleton/init behavior, but does not carry policy-facing signals (forbidden paths, migration boundary markers, or contract comments) that would make misuse harder.
findings.suggestion: Introduce boundary-level contract artifacts (API docs in-module, explicit migration notes, and import-policy enforcement) so governance is enforced by code structure rather than only repository documentation.

findings.summary: Orchestrator completion path carries an unused task ID argument
evidence: `checkOrchestratorCompletion` includes `taskIdString: string` in its signature but the parameter is not referenced anywhere in the implementation.
The call site passes `taskIdString` anyway, creating a dead argument path in completion orchestration.
findings.suggestion: Remove `taskIdString` from `checkOrchestratorCompletion` signature and update all call sites accordingly; if task ID is intended for diagnostics, explicitly use it in logs/errors inside the function instead of passing it unused.
